title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: Andhra Pradesh Class 10
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: An Andhra Pradesh farmer plants 3 crops. Probabilities of good harvest: Rice P=0.7, Cotton P=0.6, Maize P=0.8. If crops are independent, what is the probability of all three having good harvests?
questions.explanation: P(all three) = 0.7 × 0.6 × 0.8 = 0.336. This means in 3 out of 10 years, all three crops do well. Crop insurance pricing uses exactly this probability calculation. PMFBY (PM Fasal Bima Yojana) premiums are calculated using district-level crop probability data.
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: An Amaravati startup invested ₹5 lakhs. Revenue grew as GP: Year 1: ₹2L, Year 2: ₹4L, Year 3: ₹8L (doubling each year). If this trend continues, what is total revenue in 5 years?
questions.explanation: GP: a=2, r=2, n=5. Sn = a(rⁿ-1)/(r-1) = 2(2⁵-1)/(2-1) = 2×31 = 62 lakhs. GP growth = exponential growth = the dream of every startup! This is why Zomato, Swiggy, and Ola showed GP revenue curves to investors. Your AP Class 10 GP formula IS startup finance.
questions.subject: Mathematics
